The Hyundai Exter competes with the [Tata Punch](https://www.tatamotors.com/en/models/punch) in the Indian market [Source: Hyundai Exter cabin officially revealed before launch]. Other notable vehicles in the market include the [VW T-Cross](https://www.volkswagenusa.com/en/models/t-cross), [VW T-Roc](https://www.volkswagenusa.com/en/models/t-roc), [Skoda Kamiq](https://www.skoda-auto.com/en/models/kamiq), and [Renault Captur](https://www.renault.com/en/models/captur) [Source: Unknown Source].

Siemens plans to invest and expand its production in Europe, creating over 1,000 jobs [Siemens' new investment plan to create more than a 1000 jobs - CEO]. Europe is also home to Siemens' significant presence in the industrial metaverse and artificial intelligence.

Siemens is expanding its digital factory in Chengdu, China, investing €140 million in the project [Siemens Plans Investments In Manufacturing Capacity Expansion - Quick Facts]. The company is also adding a new research and development center in Chengdu.

The Competition Act and the jurisdiction of the Competition Commission of India (CCI) over Coal India Ltd. have been significant points of contention. The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the Competition Act applies to Coal India Ltd. [Source: Competition Act applies to Coal India, issue to be decided on merits: SC]. This ruling was made after Coal India Ltd. had approached the Supreme Court, arguing that the CCI had no jurisdiction over it. However, the Supreme Court dismissed Coal India Ltd.'s case, affirming the CCI's authority [Source: Competition Act Will Apply To Coal India, Says Supreme Court].

The CCI has imposed penalties on Coal India Ltd. for abusing its dominant position and for imposing unfair/discriminatory conditions in Fuel Supply Agreements [Source: Competition Act Will Apply To Coal India, Says Supreme Court; Competition Act applies to Coal India, issue to be decided on merits: SC]. This has significant implications for Coal India Ltd., a major player in the coal industry, along with other coal-related entities such as Western Coalfields Ltd., South Eastern Coal fields Ltd., and Mahanadi Coalfields Ltd.

The CCI's authority and the applicability of the Competition Act to Coal India Ltd. have been key issues in the context of Indian competition law, with the Supreme Court's rulings providing important clarifications [Source: Competition Act applies to Coal India, issue to be decided on merits: SC]. The Competition Appellate Tribunal and the Supreme Court of India have also played critical roles in shaping the regulatory landscape [Source: Unknown Source].

Overall, the relationships highlight the complex interplay between Coal India Ltd., the CCI, and the Supreme Court of India in determining the applicability of competition law to the coal sector.
